preamble: The retroflex nasal /ɳ/ surfaces as a nasalized retroflex flap [ɽ̃] in Hindi, a common allophonic variation in natural speech. This alternation reflects the phonetic properties of nasalization interacting with manner features, where the nasal airflow is maintained while the articulation becomes momentarily flap-like.

examples.note: Nasalized retroflex flap occurs as allophone of retroflex nasal in natural speech and rapid discourse

Retroflex nasal with nasalized flap realization in common Hindi words
Casual/rapid speech variant showing nasalized retroflex flap

context: Intervocalic and medial positions in Hindi, variable in rapid speech and casual discourse
allophones_note: The underlying phoneme /ɳ/ shows allophonic variation with [ɽ̃], where the flap quality is a phonetic consequence of the rapid articulation and the nasal airflow

related.label: Parallel manner change in retroflex consonants
